India's Sovereign AI Platform

## AI for all from India

Built on sovereign compute. Powered by frontier-class models.

Delivering population-scale impact.

India builds with Sarvam

## The AI Platform India Builds On

## Build anything with Sarvam APIs

Everything you need to add Indic AI to your product.

## India Can

From government services reaching 800 million citizens to enterprises transforming customer experience, AI that speaks India's languages is changing what's possible.

## Powering India's AI-first future

For Enterprise | Government | Developers

### Population-scale Applications

Building products India can use. Conversational agents fluent in India's languages. Platforms that run enterprise workflows from start to finish.

### State-of-the-Art Models

State-of-the-art models trained on sovereign data, delivering strong performance across Indian languages.

### Infrastructure to Serve Models Efficiently

A token factory built to handle complexity of model serving so teams can focus on building products, not managing infrastructure.

## Trusted by India's Largest Enterprises

Real results from real customers across BFSI, government, and enterprise.

## Enterprise-grade. Out of the box.

Compliance, control, and confidence. Not bolted on. Built in from day one.

### Forward deployed

Our engineers work alongside yours, designing agents, integrating systems, and staying until you're live. Not a handoff. A partnership.

*   Dedicated engineer from day one
*   Joint workflow design and build
*   Ongoing accuracy and cost optimisation
*   SLA-backed production support

### Deployment flexibility

Arya deploys where your data already lives. Private cloud, on-premise, hybrid, or fully air-gapped environments. Your agents run on your terms.

*   Private cloud, on-premise, or hybrid
*   Bring your own model or use ours
*   Swap vendors without rewriting workflows
*   Air-gapped deployment available

### Security and governance

Every agent action is logged and traceable. Role-based access, audit trails, and data residency controls built into the core, not retrofitted. Arya meets you where your risk team is.

SOC 2 Type II ISO 27001 DPDP compliant Role-based access Full audit trail Data residency controls

[](https://www.sarvam.ai/contact-us)

## Built to run

anywhere your business runs

## What our customers say

## Research & Updates

[](https://www.sarvam.ai/blogs)

source_review.summary: The source bundle contains the homepage of Sarvam.ai. The content provides high-level information regarding their AI platform, enterprise deployment options, and security/governance claims. No specific legal documents (e.g., full ToS, Privacy Policy, DPA) were retrieved.
limitations: Only the homepage was retrieved; no dedicated legal or governance pages were captured.
Security and compliance claims (SOC 2, ISO 27001, DPDP) are mentioned on the homepage but no supporting certificates or full policy documents were provided.

limitations: No full legal documents (Terms of Service, Privacy Policy, DPA) were visible in the admitted source bundle.

document_stack_redline.quote: SOC 2 Type II ISO 27001 DPDP compliant
document_stack_redline.contradicts: The homepage makes specific compliance claims (SOC 2, ISO 27001, DPDP), but no public legal or governance documents (ToS, Privacy Policy, DPA) are visible in the admitted evidence to support these claims.

legal_stack_review.document_stack_synthesis: The admitted public footprint for Sarvam AI is limited to the company homepage. While the homepage makes explicit claims regarding enterprise-grade compliance (SOC 2, ISO 27001, DPDP) and deployment flexibility (private cloud, on-premise, air-gapped), no core legal or governance documents—specifically ToS, Privacy Policy, DPA, AUP, or SLA—are visible in the admitted source bundle. Consequently, there is a significant gap between the public-facing security/compliance claims and the visible legal-governance stack. A founder or enterprise customer relying on these homepage claims would lack the necessary public documentation to verify the governance architecture, which is a structural gap requiring qualified legal review.

legal_stack_assessment.false_belief_note: A founder might assume that enterprise-grade claims imply the existence of standard service terms, but none are publicly verifiable.
A founder might assume that DPDP compliance claims imply a public privacy notice, but none is visible.
A founder might assume that enterprise-grade data residency controls imply a public DPA, but none is visible.

limitations: No full legal documents (ToS, Privacy Policy, DPA, AUP, SLA) were visible in the admitted source bundle.
The source bundle is limited to the homepage content.
Security and compliance claims (SOC 2, ISO 27001, DPDP) are mentioned on the homepage but no supporting certificates or full policy documents were provided.
Legal entity and HQ jurisdiction are not visible in the admitted public footprint.
